definition: The monetary policy transmission mechanism is partially broken: interest rate channel at ~60% effectiveness (mortgage lock-in), credit channel at ~80%, asset price channel at negative effectiveness (AI rally counteracted tightening with $10-15T market cap gain), exchange rate channel at ~50%, expectations channel at ~50-70%. Financial conditions loosened through 2023-2025 despite the most aggressive hiking cycle since Volcker.
evidence: AI rally added $10-15T market cap producing wealth effects of $300-750B exceeding rate tightening impulse
Goldman FCI eased ~1.0 point despite restrictive rates
Forward guidance attenuated: dovish guidance loses ~50% potency under positive correlation while hawkish retains full effect
pair_2 called this 'the single most policy-relevant insight'
The FCI loosening paradox corroborates the diagnosis
AI equity rally loosened conditions through channel orthogonal to monetary policy
Mortgage lock-in effect reduces interest rate channel effectiveness
Forward guidance asymmetrically attenuated: dovish guidance loses ~50% potency under positive correlation
Goldman FCI eased ~100bp equivalent despite 525bp of hikes
1/7 historical base rate for FCI easing during tightening (14%, 95% CI: [0.4%, 58%])
85%+ of outstanding mortgages below 5% creating mortgage lock-in with no pre-2020 parallel
AI wealth effect of $10-15T documented in market data and TIC flows
Stripping equities from FCI, remaining components show tightening consistent with prior cycles
Channel effectiveness: interest rate ~60%, credit ~80%, exchange rate ~50%, asset-price negative
2004-06 Conundrum closest analogue (7.5/10 similarity), resolved discontinuously with IG spreads 50bp to 600bp+
open_questions: Is the FCI miscalibration 40-200bp or the estimated 75-150bp?
Will the asset price channel normalize if equity valuations correct?
How should the Fed calibrate policy when its primary transmission channel is broken?
Does broken transmission explain why the correlation regime hasn't resolved despite shock mix evolution?
Is the transmission breakdown temporary (AI cycle specific) or structural?
What is the precise FCI miscalibration magnitude (40-200bp range)?
Does broken transmission prevent the correlation regime from resolving?
Is the break qualitatively novel (reversed channel) or a more severe version of historical episodes?
Does resolution depend on AI equity normalization or structural toolkit reform?
What is the most likely resolution path — historical pattern suggests longer broken leads to more violent reconnection
